text:
Once upon an autumn sunlit morning, nestled in the rolling green hills of nowhere particular, lived a tiny village. Each household had its secret garden, and in mine, my radishes grew big and colorful, though they never tasted as sweet as my daydreams.

Every kid in the village loved radishes. I mean, they were everywhere—on little plates at tea time, in every kind of snack food—and even had their own folk tale.

It was said that a long, lost time ago, when the world was just budding wild, a gardener named Radish lived here. He saved every last carrot and beetroot, all kinds of turnips too, and every single radish. He was a legend—a vegetable king!

But then, the tides changed. Humans moved in, bringing their own crops. Radish's seeds weren't chosen. No one remembered him. Soon all that remained were gnarled roots.

Every year, just before frost, children would sneak out
